decision: Option B — Part 1 ensemble raw predictions used directly. Neither Platt nor Gaussian materially improves rpAP; PP02 lift on CAID3 NOX (+0.0011) is within bootstrap noise on a 204-protein subtrack.
applies_to: All Part 3 Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 experiments — evaluation pipeline takes raw model probabilities through metrics without transformation.

platt.use_case: Model card / deployment recommendations: Platt-calibrated probabilities for thresholded operating points (improves Fmax substantially on PDB subtracks while preserving ranking-based metrics).

gaussian.use_case: Reference only — not in any active Part 3 pipeline.

rationale: +0.0011 rpAP on a 204-protein subtrack is within bootstrap noise. Locking Gaussian sigma=3.0 would add complexity to every future experiment's evaluation for zero measurable benefit. Phase 1/2 experiments compare against a clean unmodified Part 1 baseline — simpler and more honest.
